title: Why Do Socks Run Off?
poem_text:
Why do socks run off in pairs?
Do they race the washing stairs?
How does toast know when to pop?
Why won’t cereal ever stop?
Can a cloud forget to rain?
Does a shadow feel the pain?
Why do stars all twinkle slow?
Do they whisper where to go?
How does time decide to stay?
Why won’t bedtime float away?
cover_context: A pair of giggling socks sprinting down a rainbow slide while toast somersaults in mid-air.
style_prompt: Children's musical poem, acoustic guitar and lo-fi beat, 113 BPM, bouncy and playful, fun energy that channels into rhythm, clear warm vocal, every word easy to understand
